title: × Halimiocistus revolii
intro: × Halimiocistus revolii, also known as Revault's halimiocistus or × Halimiocistus revolii, is a hybrid plant in the family Cistaceae, resulting from a cross between Phlomis and Halimium species. The health benefits of × Halimiocistus revolii include its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which have been traditionally used to treat various ailments, such as digestive issues and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic effects. × Halimiocistus revolii is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to treat digestive problems, promote wound healing, and support the immune system.
taxonomy: × Halimiocistus revolii is a hybrid plant species, specifically a cross between the genus Halimiocistus and Cistus revolii. It belongs to the family Cistaceae. × Halimiocistus revolii is a low-growing, shrub-like plant native to the Mediterranean region. Its classification is further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, phylum Magnoliophyta, and class Magnoliopsida. × Halimiocistus revolii is often cultivated for its attractive foliage and ability to thrive in poor soil conditions.
benefits: × Halimiocistus revolii has been found to have potential health benefits due to its high antioxidant content, which can help protect the body against free radicals and oxidative stress. Its leaves and stems contain various bioactive compounds that may have anti-inflammatory properties, contributing to the relief of symptoms associated with conditions such as arthritis. The plant's extracts have also been studied for their potential to inhibit the growth of certain microorganisms, showcasing its antimicrobial properties. Furthermore, × Halimiocistus revolii has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including digestive issues and skin problems. More research is needed to fully explore the health benefits and potential applications of this plant.
constituents: × Halimiocistus revolii contains active constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils. These compounds contribute to its potential therapeutic and medicinal properties. The flavonoids present in the plant have been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The essential oils found in × Halimiocistus revolii may possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the effects of these active constituents on human health.
